response.justification: Szabo exceeds the minimum 15-year experience requirement and demonstrates strong technical breadth across multiple domains with proven leadership and entrepreneurial experience. However, the role specifically requires Principal-level expertise in AI strategy and recognized AI products, which are not evident in the resume. While his recent move to Acme Software to work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks is a positive signal of strategic interest in AI, it is too recent and appears infrastructure-focused rather than strategy-focused. He lacks formal advanced education in AI and has not built or shipped AI-powered products, which are preferred qualifications critical to the role's advisory and strategic responsibilities.
strengths: 20 years of progressive software engineering experience with proven ability to work across full stack, multiple languages, and diverse technical domains
Demonstrated entrepreneurial and leadership capability through founding Northwind Labs, staff-level roles, and active community engagement through teaching and open-source contributions
Recent pivot to AI infrastructure work (MCP, AI-agent tooling) at Acme Software shows strategic interest and active engagement in AI/ML ecosystem
concerns: No demonstrated expertise in AI-powered products or platforms—recent AI work is infrastructure/tooling-focused and too nascent to establish claimed AI strategy credentials
Missing preferred educational foundation: no Master's degree or PhD in AI, limiting depth in ML/AI theory and strategy required for director-level AI advisory role
Broad generalist background lacks the specialized, recognized AI expertise the role explicitly requires—experience is wide but not deep in cloud/AI strategy needed to advise customers and partners

key_factors.factor: Years of software engineering experience and leadership track record
Recent AI infrastructure work signal vs. proven AI product delivery
